info.description: This problem set focuses on fungal pathogens and antifungal agents. The difficulty may vary a lot through out the set, since this set contains a mix of four different cognitive levels of complexity (recall, understand, apply, analyze). The questions are already reviewed, so mistakes should have already been minimized, but if you come across one, you can report through the Forms in the "Info and Links".

q1.prompt: Azole antifungal agents are known to target a specific step in the fungal sterol biosynthesis pathway. Which of the following describes the function of the enzyme 14-α-demethylase in fungal cells?

options.label: Cross-linking of peptidoglycan layers in the cell wall

q2.prompt: A 12-year-old child presents with an inflammatory, scaly lesion on the scalp characterized by patches of hair loss (alopecia). Which of the following fungal species is the most probable cause of this condition?

q3.prompt: A 35-year-old male with a known history of HIV infection and a CD4 count of 85 cells/mm³ presents with fever, weight loss, and generalized skin papules, some of which exhibit central umbilication. A biopsy of a skin lesion is performed and stained with Gomori methenamine silver (GMS). Microscopic examination reveals numerous small intracellular yeast cells. Which of the following microscopic features is most characteristic of this pathogen in tissue specimens?

options.label: Single budding with a narrow base
Multiple budding in a "captain's wheel" formation
Transverse septation and division by fission
Broad-based budding with a thick cell wall

q4.prompt: A patient with invasive aspergillosis is switched from an imidazole to a different antifungal agent. Along with the new antifungal treatment, the physician also administered supplementary potassium and magnesium. What is the mechanism of action of the antifungal agent administered in this patient?

options.label: Binding to ergosterol to form membrane pores

q5.prompt: *Histoplasma capsulatum* is a dimorphic fungus that causes systemic disease. Which of the following features allows the yeast form of this pathogen to evade the host immune system during infection?

options.label: Formation of a thick polysaccharide capsule
Production of melanin in the cell wall
Secretion of keratinase to digest tissue
Survival as an intracellular pathogen within macrophages

q6.prompt: A chronic asthmatic patient presents with a worsening cough and the production of brownish mucus plugs containing eosinophils. Laboratory evaluation reveals significantly elevated serum IgE levels. What is the most likely diagnosis for this response to *Aspergillus* spp.?

q7.prompt: A 55-year-old male presents with chronic thickening, discoloration, and deformity of his toenails. He is initiated on oral terbinafine therapy. Which of the following best describes the pharmacokinetic property of this drug that makes it effective for this condition?

options.label: It is inactivated by pulmonary surfactant.
It reaches high concentrations and persists in the nail bed.
It requires a high-fat meal for any absorption.
It is primarily excreted unchanged in the urine within hours.
It requires an acidic gastric pH for absorption.

q8.prompt: A clinician is managing a neutropenic patient and orders a serum galactomannan test. Based on the provided clinical cases and descriptions, which statement correctly identifies the clinical utility of this test?

options.label: It is a pan-fungal marker used to detect all yeasts and molds.
It is specifically used to detect invasive *Aspergillus* spp. infections.
It detects the polysaccharide capsule of *Cryptococcus neoformans*.
It is the gold standard for diagnosing mucormycosis.
It is used to monitor the efficacy of terbinafine in onychomycosis.

q9.prompt: Terbinafine is an allylamine antifungal commonly used for the treatment of dermatophyte infections. What is the primary mechanism by which this medication exerts its antifungal effect?

options.label: Inhibition of squalene epoxidase leading to squalene accumulation
Binding to ergosterol to form membrane pores
Interference with microtubule function to disrupt mitosis
Conversion to 5-fluorouracil to inhibit DNA synthesis

q10.prompt: A 25-year-old male presents with several itchy, annular scaly plaques with active erythematous elevated edges on his trunk. He recently began a competitive wrestling program. What is the most likely diagnosis?
